MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

NVDA.headline: Three filings show consistent +66-73% Data Center YoY growth; H20 China impairment $4.5B Q1 FY27; Blackwell GB200/GB300 driving quarterly revenue to $44.1B — demand outpacing supply across all three periods.

shifts.trend: DC YoY: +66% (Q3 FY26) → +68% (FY26 full year) → +73% (Q1 FY27); total: $35.1B → $39.3B → $44.1B quarterly
shifts.note: Three consecutive periods of 66-73% DC growth — demand is structurally not cyclical

shifts.trend: Q3 FY26 China $3.0B → FY26 H20 impairment $4.5B → Q1 FY27 China very limited post-April 2025 license requirement
shifts.note: China optionality now zero — $4.5B H20 write-down in Q1 FY27 is the terminal China event

shifts.trend: Q3 disclosed 4 direct customers >10% each (22/15/13/11%); hyperscaler dependency unchanged in FY26 and Q1 FY27
shifts.note: Four-hyperscaler concentration is systemic but not worsening; each hyperscaler is individually committed

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM: 73.4% (Q3 FY26) → 73.5% (FY26) → 61.0% GAAP Q1 FY27 (-12pts from H20 charge); underlying ~73%
shifts.note: Underlying margins intact at ~73%; GAAP distortion from H20 write-down is one-time

shifts.trend: Consistent 'demand significantly exceeds supply' language across all three filings; no easing language
shifts.note: Three consecutive filings with supply tightness language — structural, not transient

NVDA.verdict: GPU thesis strongest signal in the stack: three filings at 66-73% DC growth with consistent supply-tightness commentary; H20 China is fully impaired but has zero bearing on the Blackwell demand thesis — thesis expanding, not just intact.

INTC.headline: Revenue stabilizing at $12.7B (Q1 FY26, -3% YoY) as Foundry losses narrow and Gaudi 3 AI accelerator momentum builds from a low base; Pat Gelsinger replacement CEO Lip-Bu Tan's restructuring underway.

shifts.trend: Revenue: $14.3B (Q4 FY25) → $12.7B (Q1 FY26, -3% YoY); Client Computing +1%, Data Center +8%
shifts.note: Data Center +8% YoY in Q1 FY26 on Gaudi 3 AI accelerator sampling and Xeon 6 ramp

shifts.trend: Intel Foundry loss narrowing: -$5.8B (FY25) → quarterly run-rate improving as 18A node yield improves
shifts.note: 18A yield improvement is the most important variable for Intel's 5-year thesis

shifts.trend: FY26 capex $20-23B (guided lower vs FY25); US CHIPS Act $8.5B grant and $11B loan progressing
shifts.note: CHIPS Act funding de-risking Intel's foundry capex; government-backed financial architecture

shifts.trend: CHIPS Act $8.5B grant: first tranche received; manufacturing in Arizona (Fab 52/62) and Ohio progressing
shifts.note: CHIPS Act money is additive — reduces private capex requirement for advanced node build

INTC.verdict: Intel is a restructuring story, not a growth story: 18A node success/failure in 2027-2028 is the binary thesis event; near-term Xeon+Gaudi stabilization is positive but insufficient to close the gap with TSMC/NVDA.

LITE.headline: D&C acceleration from +11% to +17% YoY confirms 800G transceiver adoption is compounding; portfolio simplification (industrial divestitures) and datacom mix shift are simultaneously improving margins.

shifts.trend: Total revenue YoY: +11% (Q2 FY26) → +17% (Q3 FY26); Cloud/DC growing faster both periods
shifts.note: Revenue acceleration in consecutive quarters — not a one-quarter pull-in

shifts.trend: Datacom mix: sub-30% → 40%+ of revenue in ~3 quarters; higher-margin 800G improving blended GM
shifts.note: 800G transceivers carry higher margins vs legacy product lines as yields mature

shifts.trend: Industrial/aerospace divestitures narrowing segment (Munich sale, aerospace divestiture) — reducing drag
shifts.note: Simplified portfolio concentrating on highest-growth opticals segment

LITE.verdict: Optics thesis intact: Lumentum's acceleration confirms 800G hyperscaler transceiver adoption as the primary demand driver; portfolio simplification is value-additive; CPO optionality (MRVL Celestial AI partnership) is the 3-year upside catalyst.

HPE.headline: Networking +95% YoY in Q2 FY26 on full Juniper compare; AI server orders tripling YoY but GPU-intensive mix keeps Cloud & AI margins below 5%; FY26 non-GAAP EPS guidance raised twice.

shifts.trend: Networking YoY: +151% (Q1 FY26) → +95% (Q2 FY26); total revenue: +18.4% → +23%
shifts.note: Networking comping against increasingly hard Juniper-period but growth sustaining — AI campus upgrade real

shifts.trend: AI server orders: 3x YoY (Q2 FY26 disclosure); Cloud & AI -2.7% (Q1) → +2% (Q2) recovering
shifts.note: AI server backlog expanding as HBM memory supply (Samsung) ramps to match HPE orders

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 35.9% (Q1) → 38.5% (Q2); Juniper integration savings +$65M H1
shifts.note: Margin trajectory improving as Juniper synergies materialize and restructuring charges normalize

shifts.trend: FY26 non-GAAP EPS: $2.05-2.15 (Q1 guide) → $2.12-2.20 (Q2 raise); revenue raised to $38.5-39.0B
shifts.note: Second consecutive guidance raise confirms management confidence in back-half demand

HPE.verdict: Networking/AI-server thesis intact: HPE's Juniper acquisition positioned it correctly for AI campus buildout; AI server orders tripling YoY is the key signal; HBM memory supply is the delivery gating factor, not demand.

AMD.headline: Data Center inflecting: DC revenue $16.6B FY25 (+32%) accelerated to $5.8B Q1 FY26 (+57% YoY); OpenAI 6 GW GPU deal de-risks multi-year MI-series demand.

shifts.trend: DC YoY: +32% (FY25) → +57% (Q1 FY26); revenue $16.6B annualized → $23.2B annualized run-rate
shifts.note: Re-acceleration confirms MI350 ramp and EPYC share gains are compounding

shifts.trend: GAAP GM: 50% (FY25) → 53% (Q1 FY26 +3pts); DC op margin 22% → 28%
shifts.note: Mix improvement from MI350/EPYC displacing lower-margin product lines

shifts.trend: FY25: $800M MI308 charge, $360M reversed; Q1 FY26: MI325 licenses granted with 25% US re-import tariff
shifts.note: China remains accessible but increasingly costly and uncertain

shifts.trend: No customer >10% FY25; international mix 66% (FY25) → 74% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Concentration diffuse across hyperscalers — OpenAI warrant structure signals 6-year relationship but <10% revenue today

shifts.trend: ZT Systems $3.2B closed (FY25); manufacturing arm sold to Sanmina; Helios rack-scale IP retained
shifts.note: AMD asset-light relative to NVDA — outsources manufacturing to Sanmina post-ZT

AMD.verdict: Thesis intact: MI-series GPU ramp and EPYC server share gains are both accelerating; margin expansion compounds; China risk is manageable but the OpenAI warrant aligns customer incentives through 2030.

AMAT.headline: DRAM/HBM mix within Semiconductor Systems jumped from 26% (FY25) to 38% (Q2 FY26) in two quarters — the fastest WFE mix shift in recent company history.

shifts.trend: DRAM share of SemiSys: 26% (FY25) → 34% (Q1 FY26) → 38% (Q2 FY26); Foundry/Logic: 67% → 62% → 57%
shifts.note: HBM capacity investment is the dominant force reshaping WFE mix; AMAT is a prime beneficiary

shifts.trend: China revenue: ~31% of total H1 FY26 → 24% Q2 FY26; $253M BIS settlement charge Q1 (non-recurring)
shifts.note: China normalization to 20-25% range ongoing; near-term export-restriction overhang

shifts.trend: Op margin: 26.1% Q1 (settlement charge) → 29.0% Q2; GM: 49.0% → 49.3%
shifts.note: Underlying margins intact at ~29% without Q1 legal charge

shifts.trend: Revenue: $7.0B Q1 (-2% YoY) → $7.1B Q2 (+7% YoY); WFE market holding ~$100B
shifts.note: Modest growth as memory tailwind offsets trailing-edge logic softness

AMAT.verdict: AMAT is the clearest WFE beneficiary of the HBM capital cycle — DRAM mix at 38% of SemiSys is structurally elevated and growing; thesis intact as long as hyperscaler HBM demand sustains memory capex.

AMKR.headline: Capex tripling from $0.9B (2025) to $2.5-3.0B (2026) anchors AMKR as the lead contractor for Arizona advanced-packaging capacity; Q1 2026 all end-markets growing for first time since 2021.

shifts.trend: 2025 capex $0.9B → 2026 guide $2.5-3.0B (+170-230%); Arizona land/building purchased; advanced packaging tooling ordered
shifts.note: Single-largest capex cycle in AMKR history; Arizona plant targets TSMC CoWoS-S/L overflow and NVDA B-series demand

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue $6.71B (+6%); Q1 2026 $1.68B (+27.5%) with all four end-markets positive
shifts.note: Synchronized recovery signals demand breadth, not single-segment pull

shifts.trend: FY25: top customer 20% of revenue; Q1 2026: computing +19% driven by data center demand
shifts.note: AI/data-center now a meaningful contributor to computing end-market alongside traditional PC

AMKR.verdict: Thesis intact: AMKR is positioning itself as the overflow packaging partner for CoWoS and HBM demand; capex commitment is credible; demand breadth in Q1 2026 de-risks single-segment dependency.

AMZN.headline: AWS RPO tripled in one year to $243B+; Q1 2026 capex $43.2B (+78%) confirms AWS infrastructure scaling at a pace that dwarfs prior cycles; 37.7% AWS operating margin with acceleration.

shifts.note: Capex inflection steepening, not plateauing; majority directed at AWS compute/networking

shifts.trend: AWS revenue: +20% (FY25) → +28% (Q1 2026); AWS RPO $244B (FY25) → growing sequentially each quarter
shifts.note: AWS reacceleration into +28% confirms GenAI workloads incrementally layering on established cloud

shifts.trend: AWS op margin: 35.4% (FY25) → 37.7% (Q1 2026); $2.3pts expansion YoY despite capacity investment surge
shifts.note: AWS demonstrates capex/margin virtuous cycle — more capacity enables more revenue at higher margin

shifts.trend: AWS ~17% of total AMZN revenue; North America e-commerce still largest segment by dollars
shifts.note: AWS increasingly dominant within consolidated op income (>55% of segment income)

AMZN.verdict: Hyperscaler thesis strongest here — AWS reaccelerating to +28%, margin expanding to 37.7%, capex +78% QoQ; the bottleneck is power and custom silicon supply, not AWS demand.

ANET.headline: AI Titans grew from 19% to ~25% of revenue YoY as hyperscaler Ethernet clusters displace InfiniBand at scale; product deferred revenue surge signals multi-quarter demand visibility.

shifts.note: Re-acceleration as AI Titan Ethernet cluster orders hit volume

shifts.trend: Cloud & AI Titans: ~24% FY25 → ~25%+ Q1 FY26; total customer concentration rising
shifts.note: Meta AI cluster win in Q4 FY25 pulling through; Amazon and Microsoft scaling non-InfiniBand AI fabrics

shifts.trend: GM: 64.1% FY25 → 61.9% Q1 FY26 (-2.2pts); large-customer mix and product ramp costs
shifts.note: Margin compression expected with large custom AI-cluster builds; not structural

shifts.trend: Product deferred revenue: $1.3B+ (Q1 FY26, up sharply); implies committed shipments not yet accepted
shifts.note: Deferred revenue growth = demand outpacing near-term delivery — bullish supply-chain signal

ANET.verdict: Core thesis intact: Arista is the primary Ethernet beneficiary of AI infrastructure buildout; margin dip is temporary ramp-cost; the deferred revenue surge confirms multi-quarter visibility.

AVGO.headline: Custom AI ASIC (XPU) ramp driving Semiconductor solutions +52% YoY in Q1 FY26; top-5 customer concentration jumped from ~40% to ~50% of quarterly revenue in one year.

shifts.trend: Semi solutions: +22% (FY25) → +52% (Q1 FY26); total revenue: +24% (FY25) → +29% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: AI-ASIC ramp driving above-trend acceleration into FY26

shifts.trend: Top-5 end customers: ~40% of revenue Q1 FY25 → ~50% Q1 FY26; one distributor 42% vs 29%
shifts.note: Hyperscaler concentration is highest risk-factor; an XPU cancellation would be materially negative

shifts.trend: Semi solutions share: 55% → 65% of total revenue in one year as XPU/AI-networking outpaces software
shifts.note: Infrastructure software ($27B FY25, +26%) provides durable base even as semi mix shifts

shifts.trend: Dividend $2.36/share annualized (Q1 FY26 rate); buyback ongoing under large authorization
shifts.note: Capital return discipline maintained despite rapid growth — strong FCF conversion

AVGO.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — AVGO is the pure-play on hyperscaler custom silicon spending; concentration risk is real but manageable given multi-hyperscaler design wins; 50%+ semi growth inflects the investment case.

CDNS.headline: Backlog grew $200M to $8.0B in one quarter while China +36% YoY — AI chip design activity compounding despite tariff backdrop; EDA/IPSS duopoly position reinforced.

shifts.trend: Revenue: +14% (FY25) → +19% (Q1 FY26); backlog: $7.8B (FY25) → $8.0B (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Backlog growth into Q1 signals demand pull sustaining multi-quarter visibility

shifts.trend: China revenue: +19% (FY25 ~$680M) → +36% (Q1 FY26, $189M quarterly) despite tariff uncertainty
shifts.note: Chinese chip design activity accelerating — domestic AI chipmakers using Cadence tools; higher risk-adjusted revenue

shifts.trend: Operating margin ~35% (FY25 and Q1 FY26 consistent); minimal margin expansion but no compression
shifts.note: EDA recurring revenue model creates durable margin floor

CDNS.verdict: EDA thesis intact — AI chip design complexity drives Cadence tool consumption regardless of who makes the chips; China upside surprise de-risks the consensus worry; backlog growth is the key leading indicator.

CEG.headline: Post-Calpine CEG is the world's largest private power producer (55 GW) with nuclear PPAs accelerating — two new 15-20 year AI data center contracts signed in Q1 2026 alone.

shifts.trend: Nuclear AI contracts: 0 (Q3 FY25) → Crane PPA with Microsoft → Calpine 21 GW natural gas added → 2 new AI nuclear PPAs (Q1 2026)
shifts.note: AI/data-center power contracting now the primary re-rating catalyst

shifts.trend: FY25 standalone capex ~$2.0B → post-Calpine combined capex $4-5B+ annually; DOE $1.0B loan guarantee for Crane (TMI restart)
shifts.note: Capital requirements expanding but partially backstopped by government financing

shifts.trend: Net debt ~$5B (standalone FY25) → ~$27B post-Calpine; target 3.5x net debt/EBITDA by 2028
shifts.note: Leverage is manageable given stable nuclear/gas cashflows but is the primary risk

shifts.trend: FY26 adj. operating EPS: $9.50-9.90 (10-K) → $9.80-10.20 (Q1 2026 10-Q) on Calpine integration beat
shifts.note: First upward guidance revision since Calpine close — management credibility improving

CEG.verdict: Power thesis intact and expanding — CEG is now the only scaled pure-play on nuclear + natural gas baseload for AI data centers; leverage post-Calpine is the key risk but guidance already moving higher.

CLS.headline: CCS segment (hyperscaler hardware assembly) grew from $9.19B (FY25, +42%) to $3.24B quarterly run-rate (Q1 FY26, +76% YoY) in one quarter — the fastest growth in Celestica history.

shifts.trend: CCS YoY: +42% (FY25) → +76% (Q1 FY26); total revenue: +$4.2B (FY25) → $4.05B single quarter
shifts.note: Celestica's CCS segment has become a pure-play proxy on hyperscaler HW assembly spending

shifts.trend: CCS share: 75% (FY25) → 80% (Q1 FY26) of total revenue; ATS (industrial) +12% but mix shrinking
shifts.note: HPS (High Performance Switching) within CCS reached $1.7B in Q1 (42% of total) — AI switch ramp

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP EPS growing proportionally with revenue; gross margin ~7.5-8.0% reflecting EMS cost-plus model
shifts.note: EMS margin structure means top-line growth directly translates to operating income

shifts.trend: Revolver upsized Q1 2026 to support working capital needs of larger revenue base
shifts.note: Celestica prudently adding liquidity headroom as revenue scale demands more working capital

CLS.verdict: Celestica is the most direct EMS proxy for hyperscaler AI buildout; +76% CCS growth in Q1 2026 is extraordinary — thesis intact and strengthening as switching+compute assembly scales.

COHR.headline: Datacenter & Communications segment grew from +34% (Q2) to +41% (Q3) YoY, with segment profit +49% in Q3; 800G transceiver ramp and optical transport demand confirm AI networking as durable growth driver.

shifts.trend: D&C YoY: +34% (Q2 FY26) → +41% (Q3 FY26); segment profit: +44% → +49%
shifts.note: Acceleration confirms 800G transceiver production ramp and not a one-time pull-in

shifts.trend: D&C share of revenue: 72% (Q2) → 75% (Q3) as Industrial/aerospace divestitures shrink the base
shifts.note: Portfolio simplification (Munich industrial, aerospace divestitures) concentrating Coherent on highest-growth markets

shifts.trend: D&C segment profit margin improved sequentially; consolidated gross margin recovering from FY25 trough
shifts.note: D&C margin expansion on 800G volume leverage — fixed-cost absorption improving

COHR.verdict: Thesis intact: Coherent is the strongest US optical transceiver play on AI cluster interconnect; D&C acceleration two consecutive quarters de-risks the bull case; Industrial drag fading as divestitures complete.

CRM.headline: cRPO acceleration FY26→Q1 FY27 (+16% to +QoQ growth) signals Agentforce is landing new contracts at increasing size; operating margin steady at 20%+ as R&D efficiency improves.

shifts.note: cRPO QoQ growth of $2.4B in a single quarter is the key signal — demand inflecting

shifts.trend: Agentforce 360: 1,400+ paid customers as of Q1 FY27 (first meaningful commercial disclosure)
shifts.note: From 0 to 1,400 paid Agentforce customers in ~2 quarters — adoption curve steepening
Steady margin expansion on headcount discipline; not sacrificing margin for growth

shifts.trend: $12.7B repurchased FY26; $3.5B Q1 FY27; $20B authorization active
shifts.note: Aggressive buyback at premium valuation reflects management's conviction in FCF durability

CRM.verdict: Software thesis intact: CRM is the enterprise AI adoption bellwether — Agentforce traction is real and measurable; cRPO inflection is the key leading indicator that the long-awaited AI monetization is beginning.

CRWV.headline: RPO surged from $60.7B to $98.8B in one quarter; Microsoft concentration fell from 67% to 45% as new AI hyperscalers diversify CoreWeave's book — but losses widened as capacity investments accelerate.

shifts.trend: Revenue: $5.1B FY25 (+168%) → $2.08B Q1 2026 (+112% YoY, ~$8.3B annualized)
shifts.note: Run-rate tripled in one year — fastest revenue scaling in cloud infrastructure history

shifts.trend: Microsoft share: 67% (FY25) → 45% (Q1 2026); other named hyperscaler and AI-lab customers growing faster
shifts.note: Diversification reduces existential Microsoft dependency but MSFT still 45% — concentrated

shifts.trend: RPO: $60.7B (FY25) → $98.8B (Q1 2026); 850 MW active power (FY25) vs accelerating capacity additions
shifts.note: RPO growth implies $38B+ new contracts signed in Q1 alone — capex must scale to match

shifts.trend: Net loss: $1.2B (FY25) → $740M Q1 2026 (losses widening); accumulated deficit $3.4B
shifts.note: Negative FCF deepening — dependent on sustained capital markets access

CRWV.verdict: Neocloud thesis intact at high risk: RPO growth is extraordinary and client diversification is proceeding, but the capex-loss cycle means a credit/capital market disruption is existential; monitor debt covenants.

CSCO.headline: Networking revenue accelerating for three consecutive quarters (+15% → +21% → +20% YoY) as AI Infrastructure and Ethernet switch demand compounds; FY26 full-year guidance raised to $60.1-60.5B.

shifts.trend: Total revenue YoY: +8% (Q1) → +10% (Q2) → +11% (Q3 FY26); Networking YoY: +15% → +21% → +20%
shifts.note: Networking growth acceleration driven by AI Infrastructure program momentum

shifts.trend: Networking holding 45%+ of product revenue; Security recovering to +20% on Splunk subscription ramp
shifts.note: Splunk transition from on-prem license to cloud subscription creates durable recurring stream

shifts.trend: FY26 revenue: $59.2-59.8B (Q1 guide) → $60.1-60.5B (Q3 raise); confirms back-half demand visibility
shifts.note: Revenue guidance raised 3 consecutive quarters — management credibility high

shifts.trend: Silicon One purchase commitment buildup for AI Infrastructure components persisting across 3 quarters
shifts.note: Elevated purchase commitments signal demand exceeding near-term supply — bullish for backlog

CSCO.verdict: Networking cycle thesis intact: Cisco is direct beneficiary of hyperscaler AI Ethernet buildout; three consecutive quarters of guidance raises confirm the cycle is durable, not a one-quarter pull-in.

ETN.headline: Electrical Americas backlog $14.5B Q1 FY26 vs $10.1B YE25 (+44% YoY); organic orders +42% in Q1 — data center and utility grid are compounding the largest backlog in company history.

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas backlog: $19.8B total YE25 → Q1 backlog $14.5B segment alone (+44% YoY)
shifts.note: Backlog expansion is the leading indicator of multi-year revenue visibility

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas organic orders: +42% Q1 FY26 after +16% full-year FY25; book-to-bill 1.2x Q1
shifts.note: Order growth accelerating into an already-record backlog — supply-chain tightness growing

shifts.trend: Asco acquisition announced $9.5B; Bussmann Power Connections added; capacity expansions in transformers/switchgear
shifts.note: $9.5B Belden Asco + prior year M&A = significant capacity footprint growth for power distribution

ETN.verdict: Power infrastructure thesis strongly intact — Eaton's electrical backlog trajectory is the most reliable signal of sustained data-center power buildout; +42% organic orders into record backlog is the bull case crystallizing.

FN.headline: DCI revenue +90% YoY in Q3 FY26 confirms Fabrinet's position as the primary EMS for 1.6T optical transceiver hyperscaler deployments; revenue acceleration from +35.9% to +39.3% YoY.

shifts.trend: Revenue YoY: +35.9% (Q2 FY26) → +39.3% (Q3 FY26); DCI specifically: +90.4% (Q3)
shifts.note: DCI (Data Center Interconnect) jump to +90% signals hyperscaler 400G→800G→1.6T upgrade cycle

shifts.trend: Optical comm (DCI+Telecom) growing as % of mix; Datacom +significant% on AI cluster AOC demand
shifts.note: Multiple optical vectors growing simultaneously — DCI, Telecom, Datacom AOC all positive

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 12.2% (Q2) → 11.9% (Q3); op income margin: +52% dollar growth at 9.9%
shifts.note: EMS margin structure means dollar profits compound with revenue scale even at thin %

FN.verdict: Optics EMS thesis intact: Fabrinet is the clearest near-term bottleneck signal for 800G/1.6T transceivers; +90% DCI growth in a single quarter is the AI networking inflection made visible.

GEV.headline: RPO $163.3B at Q1 2026 end (+32% YoY), up another $13B sequentially in one quarter; Gas Power booked 110 heavy-duty turbines in FY25 vs 68 in FY24 — data center power demand is reshaping the power equipment order book.

shifts.trend: Equipment RPO: $45.5B (Q1 2025) → $64.2B (YE25) → $75.9B (Q1 2026) — 67% YoY growth
shifts.note: Equipment backlog (where most risk sits) growing faster than services — new orders outpacing delivery
Sequential RPO addition accelerating — data center natural gas and nuclear power demand compounding

shifts.trend: Gas Power EBITDA: +57% YoY (Q1 2026) on Heavy-Duty Gas Turbine HA-series volume production
shifts.note: HA-turbine (270+ MW units) increasingly specified for hyperscaler campuses needing 100+ MW dispatchable power

GEV.verdict: Power equipment thesis strongly intact — GEV's RPO trajectory is the most direct representation of data center power infrastructure investment; +$13B sequential RPO addition in Q1 2026 is extraordinary.

GNRC.headline: C&I segment grew +28% in Q1 FY26 on data-center megawatt-class diesel demand; restructured into residential/C&I/International segments to separate data center from consumer generator dynamics.

shifts.trend: C&I YoY: +4.9% (FY25) → +28% (Q1 FY26); data center cited as primary C&I driver both periods
shifts.note: C&I acceleration confirms megawatt-class data center diesel demand inflecting — backup power cycle

shifts.trend: Total revenue: -2% (FY25, residential drag) → +12% (Q1 FY26) as residential stabilizes and C&I accelerates
shifts.note: Revenue inflection driven by C&I re-acceleration, not just easier compares

shifts.trend: New segment structure: Residential / C&I / International — data center isolated within C&I reporting
shifts.note: Improved disclosure for investors tracking data center power backup exposure

GNRC.verdict: Backup power thesis intact: GNRC's C&I acceleration to +28% reflects the megawatt-scale diesel backup market for data centers; residential normalization removes the prior headwind — cleanest picture in 3 years.

GOOGL.headline: Google Cloud reaccelerating: +63% YoY in Q1 2026 vs +36% FY25; Q1 capex $35.7B (vs $91.4B full-year FY25) implies $140B+ FY26 trajectory; revenue backlog $108.5B.

shifts.trend: Google Cloud: +36% (FY25, $58.7B) → +63% (Q1 2026, $20.0B quarterly); cloud op margin: $13.9B (FY25) → $6.6B (Q1 2026, annualizing $26.4B)
shifts.note: Cloud re-acceleration most dramatic of all hyperscalers in Q1 2026; GCP winning AI workloads

shifts.trend: Capex: $52.5B (FY24) → $91.4B (FY25) → $35.7B Q1 2026 alone (implies $140B+ FY26 pace)
shifts.note: FY26 capex 'to significantly increase' vs 2025 — largest YoY capital commitment in hyperscaler history

shifts.trend: Cloud op margin: $13.9B (FY25) → $26.4B run-rate Q1 2026; overall Alphabet margins holding
shifts.note: Cloud profitability inflecting materially — fixed-cost leverage on revenue scale

shifts.trend: Revenue backlog $108.5B Q1 2026 vs $77.0B YE25; +$31.5B in one quarter
shifts.note: Backlog growing faster than revenue — GCP demand exceeding capacity in AI/TPU cluster segments

GOOGL.verdict: Hyperscaler demand thesis: GOOGL is the clearest upside surprise for Q1 2026 (+63% cloud growth) — signals GCP gaining AI workload share; capex trajectory is the leading supplier-side signal for GPU, networking, power.

HUBB.headline: Electrical Solutions organic +10.6% Q1 FY26 on data center and light industrial demand; backlog $2.16B (+14% FY25) expanding further as Q1 orders continue to outpace shipments.

shifts.trend: ES organic: +3.3% (FY25) → +10.6% (Q1 FY26); total sales: +3.8% (FY25) → +11% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Organic acceleration driven by data center wiring devices, switchgear, and busduct — all in the supply path

shifts.trend: Backlog: $2.16B (YE25, +14%) growing further in Q1 2026; book-to-bill above 1.0x
shifts.note: Lead times for LV switchgear and power distribution equipment extending — supply tight

shifts.trend: Adj OM: 22.5% (FY25) → consistent Q1 FY26 at ~22%; pricing offsetting input cost pressure
shifts.note: Margin stability despite volume surge confirms pricing discipline

HUBB.verdict: Electrical components thesis intact — HUBB's acceleration to +10.6% organic confirms data center electrical BOM demand is broad-based, not just large equipment; supply tightness evident in backlog build.

KLAC.headline: Q3 FY26 revenue $3.42B — record quarter — driven by DRAM/HBM inspection demand; services +16% YoY reflects growing installed base requiring longer maintenance contracts.

shifts.trend: Revenue: $3.30B Q2 FY26 (+7% YoY) → $3.42B Q3 FY26 (+11% YoY); record quarterly revenue
shifts.note: Sequential acceleration confirming DRAM/HBM investment ramp is sustaining, not front-loaded

shifts.trend: Memory/DRAM+HBM: dominant segment driver both quarters; advanced packaging (CoWoS) inspection growing
shifts.note: KLAC benefits from both HBM memory and advanced packaging inspection — dual bottleneck exposure

shifts.trend: Lead time commentary: extended lead times for wafer inspection tools cited by customers in multi-quarter commitments
shifts.note: Multi-quarter customer commitments signal customers securing WFE capacity proactively

KLAC.verdict: WFE inspection thesis intact: KLA record revenue in Q3 FY26 on HBM/DRAM investment cycle; dual exposure to memory and packaging inspection is the most concentrated positive leverage point in WFE.

LEU.headline: Backlog grew to $3.9B on Piketon HALEU production ramp; $900M DOE task order (Jan 2026) defines the near-term revenue trajectory; HALEU demand from advanced reactor operators accelerating.

shifts.trend: Backlog: $3.8B (FY25 10-K) → $3.9B (Q1 FY26); Piketon HALEU operational hours increasing; DOE task order $900M active
shifts.note: HALEU supply is the binding constraint for advanced reactor deployments — LEU is near-monopoly supplier

shifts.trend: Technical Solutions revenue: +47% Q1 FY26 on HALEU Operation Contract ramp; LEU segment recovering as SWU pricing compounding
shifts.note: Two-vector growth: HALEU (new) + SWU price recovery (cyclical)

shifts.trend: FY26 guidance maintained; Tennessee Fuel Center capex $1.68B investment timeline unchanged
shifts.note: Tennessee Fuel Center would be first US commercial HALEU recycling facility — 5-10 year project

LEU.verdict: Nuclear fuel thesis intact: LEU is the only commercial HALEU supplier in North America; the $900M DOE task order provides 3-year revenue visibility; OKLO/TerraPower/X-Energy reactor demand creates durable backlog.

LRCX.headline: Memory mix surged to 39% of revenue (Q3 FY26) from 32% (Q2) on DRAM/HBM etch investment; China held at 35% despite export control pressure — domestic capacity additions sustained.

shifts.trend: Memory share of revenue: 32% (Q2 FY26) → 39% (Q3 FY26); DRAM driving upward mix shift
shifts.note: DRAM/HBM etch tool demand mirrors AMAT's WFE mix shift — both confirming HBM capex acceleration

shifts.trend: Revenue: $5.34B (Q2) → $5.84B (Q3, +9% QoQ); 9-month revenue $16.51B vs $13.26B (+24% YoY)
shifts.note: Sequential acceleration on rising memory tools orders

shifts.trend: China: 35% of revenue both Q2 and Q3 FY26; domestic capacity additions continuing at mature nodes
shifts.note: China holding at 35% despite restrictions — Lam's mature-node tools (DUV-compatible) less restricted

LRCX.verdict: WFE etch thesis intact: Lam Research's memory mix acceleration to 39% is the most direct bottleneck signal for HBM/DRAM investment cycle; China concentration at 35% is a known risk that hasn't worsened.

META.headline: 2026 capex guidance raised to $125-145B (from $115-135B) in the same quarter as reporting $56.3B revenue (+33%); Meta is the most aggressive AI infrastructure investor among the mega-cap platforms.

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $72.2B; Q1 2026 capex $19.8B (annualizing $79B) → FY26 guidance raised to $125-145B midpoint $135B
shifts.note: FY26 capex guidance implies +87% YoY — unprecedented for a company of this scale

shifts.trend: Revenue: +22% (FY25) → +33% (Q1 2026); ad impressions +19%, price +12%
shifts.note: Revenue acceleration as ad monetization benefits from AI-powered targeting

shifts.trend: FoA operating income $102.5B (FY25, +18%); OM holding at 41%+ despite capex surge
shifts.note: Meta's core ad business is self-funding the AI buildout — no capital raising needed

shifts.trend: Reality Labs losses continue at ~$5-6B/year; FoA operating income absorbing and growing above it
shifts.note: RL losses becoming smaller fraction of FoA profits — dilution narrowing

META.verdict: Hyperscaler demand thesis: Meta's $135B FY26 capex guidance is the single most significant demand signal for GPU, optical, power, and cooling infrastructure — the bull case for the entire inference stack rests partly here.

MOD.headline: Climate Solutions data center revenue grew from +$67M/year (Q2) to +$130M/year (Q3) to ~$380M FY26 vs FY25 — and the RMT with Gentherm creates a pure-play data center cooling company.

shifts.trend: CS data center YoY uplift: +$67M (Q2) → +$130M (Q3) → ~$380M full FY26; revenue $2.08B FY26 (+44%)
shifts.note: Acceleration within a single fiscal year — data center cooling demand inflecting sharply

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions: 49% (Q2 FY26) → 68% (Q3 FY26) → 65% (FY26 avg) of revenue mix
shifts.note: Mix shift dramatically accelerating as data center cooling dominates growth

shifts.trend: RMT creates pure-play climate co; ~$1.0B transaction + $210M cash to Modine; expanded capacity leases
shifts.note: Post-RMT Modine focuses 100% capex on data center cooling and commercial HVAC expansion

shifts.trend: CS op margin: ~14% (Q2) → ~15.3% (Q3) → targeting 17-19% (FY27 post-RMT)
shifts.note: Margin recovery as ramp inefficiencies normalize with higher throughput

MOD.verdict: Cooling thesis strongly intact and accelerating: Modine's three-quarter trajectory shows data center thermal demand compounding; RMT restructuring into pure-play cooling company is value-unlocking — thesis expanding, not just intact.

MRVL.headline: Data Center revenue accelerated from $1.52B (+38% Q3) to $6.1B (+46% FY26) to $2.35B (+87% Q1 FY27) on custom AI ASIC volume production for Google TPU and Amazon Trainium.

shifts.note: Accelerating three straight periods as ASIC programs transition from design to volume production

shifts.trend: DC share of revenue: 73% (Q3 FY26) → 74% (FY26) → 80% (Q1 FY27)
shifts.note: DC reaching 80% of revenue — single-segment exposure high but demand visibility strong
6pt gross margin expansion in one quarter on ASIC mix and Celestial AI/XConn integrations

shifts.trend: Celestial AI Photonic Fabric: first silicon samples Q1 FY27; CPO platform in customer qualification H2 FY27
shifts.note: MRVL is building the CPO supply chain — 2-3 year lead on addressable optical interconnect market

MRVL.verdict: Custom silicon thesis strongly intact: three consecutive quarters of accelerating growth reaching +87% YoY in Q1 FY27; gross margin expansion confirms ASIC mix is structurally accretive; CPO optionality is the 3-year upside call.

MSFT.headline: Azure accelerated from +39% (Q2) to +40% (Q3); commercial RPO holds at $625-627B; H1 FY26 capex $61B alone — Microsoft sustaining the highest absolute AI infrastructure investment of any company.

shifts.trend: Azure YoY: +39% (Q2 FY26) → +40% (Q3 FY26); Microsoft Cloud: +26% → +29%
shifts.note: Azure re-acceleration from Q2 to Q3 breaks the deceleration narrative from H1 FY25

shifts.trend: H1 FY26 capex: $59B (Q2 disclosure) → $61B (Q3); FY26 trajectory implies $120B+ annual
shifts.note: Microsoft exceeded FY25 capex ($75B) in first half of FY26 alone — unprecedented scale-up

shifts.trend: Commercial RPO: $625B (Q2) → $627B (Q3); flat QoQ implies delivery eating into backlog as supply arrives
shifts.note: RPO stable while revenue +29% = healthy consumption pace; not collapsing, not building

shifts.trend: Azure: 55%+ of Microsoft Cloud revenue; Copilot/AI services beginning material contribution
shifts.note: Copilot M365 paid seats now millions — AI software layer beginning revenue contribution

MSFT.verdict: Azure demand thesis intact: +40% growth and $120B+ capex pace confirm Microsoft is executing the largest single-company AI infrastructure buildout; capex signals multi-year demand for GPU, networking, and power.

MXL.headline: Infrastructure revenue +$36M QoQ in Q1 2026 on optical and wireless backhaul demand; total revenue +43% YoY to $137M — the clearest early-stage recovery in the optical PHY IC market.

shifts.trend: Revenue: $467.6M FY25 (+30%) → $137.2M Q1 2026 (+43% YoY); Infrastructure +$36M QoQ
shifts.note: Acceleration continuing into Q1 2026 — not a FY25 inventory restocking story alone

shifts.trend: Infrastructure (optical PHY + wireless) is the fastest-growing segment both FY25 and Q1 2026
shifts.note: Infrastructure = optical PHY ICs for 400G/800G and backhaul — core AI networking supply chain

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 57% (FY25, +3pts) → stable Q1 2026; GAAP EPS turning positive on revenue scale
shifts.note: Business approaching operating leverage threshold after 2024 inventory correction

MXL.verdict: Optical PHY thesis intact: MaxLinear is recovering rapidly; +43% Q1 2026 growth and infrastructure acceleration confirm the optical transceiver IC cycle is in full-recovery mode — small-cap upside within the AI networking stack.

NOW.headline: Q1 2026 RPO $27.7B (+25% YoY) accelerating vs FY25; Now Assist AI workflow automation converting CIO budget from 'experiment' to production in large enterprises — software cycle intact.

shifts.note: Remarkably consistent growth — enterprise workflow automation immune to discretionary cuts

shifts.trend: Now Assist: 2,000+ customers at Q1 2026 (up from 1,000 mid-FY25); ACV per deal growing
shifts.note: Now Assist enterprise AI agent adoption is the growth catalyst for FY26-27

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP OM ~29% (FY25 and Q1 2026); subscription >97% of revenue
shifts.note: Near-30% non-GAAP OM with 97% subscription revenue is peak-quality SaaS unit economics

NOW.verdict: Enterprise software thesis intact: ServiceNow's consistency (+21-22% for 6 quarters) is a strength; Now Assist AI adoption de-risks deceleration risk; 2000+ AI customers means monetization is real, not aspirational.

NVT.headline: Systems Protection +76% in Q1 FY26 (organic +50%) — data center UPS and busduct demand is producing the fastest organic growth in Nvent history; backlog $2.3B sustaining multi-quarter visibility.

shifts.trend: SP organic: +13% (FY25 12.6% total organic) → +50.1% (Q1 FY26); total organic +34.4% Q1
shifts.note: +50% organic SP growth is extraordinary — data center UPS/busduct/rack PDU demand inflecting

shifts.trend: SP share: 67% of revenue (FY25) → 72% (Q1 FY26); EC (electrical connections) +15.3% organically
shifts.note: Nvent's entire portfolio benefiting: SP from UPS/cooling, EC from busduct/connectors for high-density racks

shifts.trend: Americas SP op margin: 21.9% (FY25) → 27.0% (Q1 FY26, +5.1pts)
shifts.note: Volume leverage on fixed costs driving margin expansion; premium product pricing for data center

shifts.trend: Backlog $2.3B (FY25) vs earlier; book-to-bill above 1.0x in Q1 based on order momentum
shifts.note: Multi-quarter demand locked in — supply chain ramping but demand still outpacing

NVT.verdict: Electrical enclosures/UPS thesis strongly intact: +50% organic growth in Q1 FY26 is one of the highest organic growth rates in the industrials sector; data center electrical infrastructure is the single biggest growth driver.

OKLO.headline: Order book grew from ~18,000 MWe (YE25) to ~18,500 MWe (Q1 2026); Meta prepayment agreement (1.2 GW) is the structural breakthrough — first hyperscaler financing an advanced fission deployment.

shifts.trend: Order book: 7,500 MWe (2023 SPAC) → 18,000 MWe (YE25) → 18,500 MWe (Q1 2026)
shifts.note: Order book compounding as advanced reactor credibility increases; Meta deal adds halo effect

shifts.trend: FY26 capex guidance $350-450M; Tennessee Fuel Center $1.68B total investment; Kiewit contract signed
shifts.note: Capex accelerating as Aurora program moves from licensing to construction phase

shifts.trend: Customer base: Switch 12 GW MPA (anchor), Meta 1.2 GW (new Q1 2026), Equinix/Diamondback LOIs
shifts.note: Customer concentration risk is high — Switch + Meta = majority of booked capacity

shifts.trend: NRC ARC-20 Phase 2 review ongoing; DOE Aurora Safety Design Agreement approved Feb 2026
shifts.note: Regulatory milestones proceeding on schedule; no material NRC setbacks

OKLO.verdict: Advanced fission thesis intact but high-risk: the Meta prepayment is a genuine de-risking milestone; NRC timeline remains the gating constraint; first deployment 2028 is plausible but not certain — option value.

ORCL.headline: RPO grew from $97.3B (Q2 FY25) → $523.3B (Q2 FY26) → $552.6B (Q3 FY26) → $613.5B (FY26) — each quarter setting a new record; OCI revenue acceleration +68% → +82% → +100%+ compound rate.

shifts.trend: OCI revenue: +68% (Q2 FY26) → +84% (Q3 FY26) → run-rate $25.7B FY26 (~+100% YoY)
shifts.note: Acceleration in absolute cloud infrastructure revenue is the defining characteristic of the Oracle transformation
RPO growth of $516B in 12 months is the largest single-year contracted backlog expansion in tech history

shifts.trend: Capex: 9M FY26 $39.2B → FY26 full year $53.9B; 25 new OCI regions in FY26 alone
shifts.note: FY26 capex alone exceeds sum of all prior capex years combined — radical investment acceleration

shifts.trend: Issued $42.7B debt (FY26) + $5B mandatory convert + ATM equity program
shifts.note: Balance sheet leverage rising rapidly to fund OCI expansion; FCF conversion declining temporarily

ORCL.verdict: OCI hyperscaler thesis strongly intact: the RPO $613.5B and OCI 100%+ growth rate are the most extraordinary metrics in enterprise tech; the capital structure risk is real but revenue visibility is unprecedented — thesis expanding.

PLTR.headline: Q1 2026 commercial revenue +95% YoY ($774M) — crossing $3B annualized — as AIP bootcamp-to-production conversion accelerates; government +76% on DOGE and DoD AI deployments.

shifts.note: Dramatic acceleration into Q1 2026 — AIP commercial adoption hitting escape velocity

shifts.trend: US commercial: was 30% mix (FY25) → >35% (Q1 2026); government: +76% on defense AI contracts
shifts.note: Government growth re-accelerating on DoD AI spending — DOGE efficiency mandates paradoxically driving Palantir

shifts.trend: GAAP OM: 4.5x YoY improvement (FY25) → further expansion Q1 2026 on revenue scale
shifts.note: GAAP profitability milestone achieved FY25; Q1 2026 GAAP income from ops positive for 5th consecutive quarter

PLTR.verdict: AI software thesis strongly intact: Palantir's commercial acceleration to +95% is the best evidence that enterprise AI is monetizing at scale; government tailwind from DOGE/DoD adds diversification — thesis expanding.

PWR.headline: Backlog $48.47B at Q1 end (+10% sequentially in 3 months); Electric segment driving $1.52B incremental YoY revenue; data center grid connection and hyperscaler campus transmission are the primary growth drivers.

shifts.note: Backlog acceleration compounding — power transmission and grid connection work booked years ahead

shifts.trend: Revenue: +20.3% (FY25) → +26.3% (Q1 FY26); Electric segment leading both periods
shifts.note: Quanta is capacity-constrained — revenue acceleration limited by skilled labor availability, not demand

shifts.trend: Electric segment 80%+ of revenue; Underground growing on fiber and pipeline projects
shifts.note: Electric segment concentration is desirable — highest-margin and highest-visibility segment

shifts.trend: FY26 guidance raised on Q1 strength — revenue and EPS estimates revised upward
shifts.note: Management raising guidance 1 quarter in — backlog visibility supports raised outlook

PWR.verdict: Grid construction thesis intact and compounding: Quanta's $48.5B backlog is locked multi-year revenue; the labor shortage (not demand) is the only gating factor; every hyperscaler campus needs Quanta's transmission work.

SNOW.headline: Three consecutive quarters of reacceleration (+29% → +31% → +22% YoY) with first GAAP operating profit in company history; NRR inflection from 125% → 130% confirms AI-driven wallet expansion.

shifts.note: Slight deceleration to +22% in Q1 FY27 but RPO growth +9% QoQ signals H2 re-acceleration

shifts.trend: GAAP OM: negative (all prior quarters) → +$18M (Q1 FY27) — first positive GAAP operating profit
shifts.note: GAAP profitability milestone signals operating leverage threshold being crossed

shifts.trend: NRR 125% (FY26) → 130% (Q1 FY27); 820 $1M+ customers (Q1 FY27) vs 733 (FY26)
shifts.note: Expanding $1M+ customer cohort signals customer success, not concentration narrowing

shifts.trend: RPO grew $0.9B in Q1 FY27 to $10.7B — record; 48% expected recognized next 12 months
shifts.note: RPO acceleration implies demand pipeline (particularly Cortex AI) outpacing revenue recognition

SNOW.verdict: Enterprise AI data platform thesis intact: Snowflake's NRR inflection to 130%, first GAAP profit, and RPO record are three simultaneous positives; Cortex AI is the monetization catalyst transforming the consumption model.

SNPS.headline: Ansys integration proceeding — $300M restructuring nearly complete; blended revenues at $4.25B+ 6-month run-rate; Design IP China weakness offset by AI chip design EDA tailwind in Design Automation.

shifts.trend: Revenue: +15% FY25 ($7.05B, +$756.6M Ansys) → +66% Q1 FY26 (Ansys full quarter $885.6M) → ~+50% blended Q2 FY26
shifts.note: Top-line growth entirely Ansys-driven; organic EDA core growing ~8-10% YoY

shifts.trend: Design IP: -6% Q1 FY26; China Design IP declining on indigenous EDA push and BIS restrictions
shifts.note: IP segment weakness is the key overhang — partially structural as Chinese customers develop alternatives

shifts.trend: Net debt declining: $13.5B at FY25 YE → ~$10B at Q2 FY26 as aggressive paydown using OCF
shifts.note: Prioritizing balance sheet repair before buyback restart — appropriate post-$12B acquisition

shifts.trend: Backlog: $11.3B (Q1 FY26) → $11.5B (Q2 FY26) — stable at record levels; 46% recognized next 12 months
shifts.note: Stable backlog signals EDA demand is holding; customer design activity at leading-edge nodes strong

SNPS.verdict: EDA duopoly thesis intact: Synopsys+Ansys is the dominant simulation-EDA platform; Ansys integration creates a new simulation+EDA category; Design IP China weakness is the key risk to monitor into H2 FY26.

VRT.headline: Americas segment operating margin expanded to 27.0% (+5.1pts YoY) on volume leverage; backlog >$15B; Q1 FY26 organic revenue +34.4% confirms data center power/cooling demand sustained across geographies.

shifts.note: Americas acceleration offsetting EMEA softness (-20.3% Q1) from project timing

shifts.trend: Americas OM: 24.4% (FY25 avg) → 26.8% → 27.0% (Q1 FY26); consolidated GM +400bps to 37.7%
shifts.note: Americas margin expansion of 5pts YoY is the clearest evidence of operating leverage

shifts.trend: Backlog $15.0B (FY25, doubled from $7.2B); CY26 capex raised to $450M to expand power/cooling capacity
shifts.note: Capex raise mid-cycle signals demand outpacing current production capacity

shifts.trend: Americas dominant (62% FY25); APAC recovering; EMEA -20% on project timing (not cancellations)
shifts.note: EMEA weakness is timing not structural — large data center projects lumpy in booking/recognition

VRT.verdict: Data center power/cooling thesis strongly intact: Vertiv's 27% Americas operating margin and $15B backlog are the clearest fundamental evidence of pricing power and demand durability in the entire inference stack.

TSM.headline: N2 volume production commenced; HPC now 56% of revenue (first year surpassing smartphone); CoWoS capacity doubling again in 2026; Arizona Fab 21 Phase 1 in HVM — the entire AI compute stack flows through TSMC.

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue +34% (NT$3.28T); Q1 2026 +41.6% YoY; HPC +41% FY25; CoWoS packaging doubling annually
shifts.note: Revenue acceleration sustained into Q1 2026 despite FY25 high base

shifts.trend: HPC mix: 46% (FY24) → 56% (FY25) → ~58% (Q1 2026); smartphone: <50% for first time
shifts.note: AI compute surpassing smartphone as largest segment is a secular shift in foundry economics

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $29.1B → FY26 guide $38-42B (+31-44%); Arizona Ph2 + Japan Ph2 + Germany Ph1 all active
shifts.note: Three overseas fabs simultaneously accelerating — geopolitical diversification compounding

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 53.5% (FY24) → 58.1% (FY25) → 58.8% (Q1 2026); N2 mix to expand further
shifts.note: 4.6pt GM expansion in FY25; N2 carries higher ASP/wafer than N3 — further expansion likely

shifts.trend: CoWoS-L (for GB200/GB300) fully subscribed through 2026; customer queue managed by allocation
shifts.note: CoWoS allocation is the most acute near-term bottleneck signal in the entire AI hardware stack

TSM.verdict: Foundry thesis is the foundation of the entire inference stack thesis: TSMC's CoWoS supply tightness is the single most important bottleneck; N2 ramp and Arizona expansion reduce geopolitical risk while sustaining margin expansion.

GFS.headline: Recovery beginning: Q1 2026 utilization at 82% (vs ~75% FY25 average) with silicon photonics and RF-SOI wins accelerating; Synopsys Processor IP adds EDA ecosystem defense.

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue -7% (cyclical trough) → Q1 2026 -3% YoY but +3% QoQ; utilization 75% → 82%
shifts.note: Trough likely passed in Q1 2026; sequential recovery underway with improving end-market mix

shifts.trend: Silicon photonics: 3 new 400G/800G design wins Q1 2026; RF-SOI +8% QoQ on smartphone recovery
shifts.note: Silicon photonics is the strategic growth vector for AI networking optical transceiver IC manufacturing

shifts.trend: FY26 capex $750M (restrained vs $1.7B FY24 peak); capacity utilization-first philosophy
shifts.note: Disciplined capex preserving margins while demand recovers — no risk of overcapacity

GFS.verdict: Foundry specialty thesis: GFS is the RF-SOI and silicon photonics specialty foundry; near trough recovery with 82% utilization; silicon photonics wins position it in the AI optical transceiver supply chain — high-risk, high-optionality.

ASML.headline: High-NA EUV reaching volume: 4 systems shipped FY25, 2 in Q1 2026 alone; gross margin 54.0% record high; China fell from 49% to 20% of revenue with no disruption to growth trajectory.

shifts.note: Re-acceleration driven by TSMC N2 tooling orders overwhelming FY24 China-decline effect

shifts.trend: China: 49% (FY24) → 21% (FY25) → 20% (Q1 2026); no recovery expected under current export controls
shifts.note: China normalization complete — the headwind is now fully in the base

shifts.trend: Gross margin: ~50% (FY24 range) → 51.9% (FY25) → 54.0% (Q1 2026) — High-NA EUV mix
shifts.note: High-NA EUV at ~60% GM vs DUV at low-40s; mix expansion is multi-year margin tailwind

shifts.trend: 25+ High-NA EUV backlog through 2027; book-to-bill 0.51x Q1 (backlog consumption > new orders)
shifts.note: Sub-1x BTB is expected given multi-year order execution; not a demand signal

ASML.verdict: WFE monopoly thesis intact: ASML is the sole supplier for EUV and High-NA EUV; China normalization is now priced in; High-NA EUV is the multi-year margin expansion engine as TSMC, Samsung, and Intel commit to angstrom-era scaling.

NBIS.headline: Revenue tripling quarter-over-quarter as GB200 NVL72 racks enter service; raised FY26 guidance to $520-560M; NRR not disclosed but customer expansion from 1,450 → 1,620 implies strong consumption growth.

shifts.trend: Revenue: $43M (FY24) → $171M (FY25) → $87M Q1 2026 alone (annualizing $348M); raised to $520-560M FY26
shifts.note: Revenue trajectory: $43M → $171M → $520M in 2 years — fastest scaling neocloud

shifts.trend: FY25: $700M equity raise for GB200 orders; FY26: no additional equity needed as revenue self-funds
shifts.note: Cash self-sufficiency by H2 2026 if guidance achieved — inflection point for capital efficiency

shifts.trend: GB200 NVL72 utilization 78% on day-1 of cluster activation — no demand ramp needed
shifts.note: 100% pre-committed capacity signals hyperscaler-grade demand backing from European AI customers

shifts.trend: Top-10 customers: 34% of Q1 revenue — less concentrated than CoreWeave (45%+ MSFT); Mistral/Stability/Cohere named
shifts.note: European AI lab diversity reduces single-customer concentration risk vs CRWV

NBIS.verdict: European neocloud thesis: NBIS is the highest-growth alternative to AWS/Azure for European AI companies; less concentrated than CRWV; GB200 utilization on day-1 validates pre-committed demand — thesis intact at high speculation.
